sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.82 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: Is there a function$f:{ N} { N}$such that for any prime$p$, if$p^{f(n)}$divides the order of a finite group$G$, then$p^n$divides the order of${ Aut}\,G$? { R.\,M.\,Patne} Yes, there is. This was established in (J.\,A.\,Green, Proc. Roy. Soc. London Ser.~A., { 237} (1956), 574--581), which improved a previous result with a function depending on$p$in (W.\,Ledermann, B.\,H.\,Neumann, Proc. Roy. Soc. ...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
